title: Can I throw this one out there This has really bothered me for about 2 weeks
display_title: Can I throw this one out there This has really bothered me for about 2 weeks
wikitext:
[]: Can I throw this one out there? This has really bothered me for about 2 weeks.             Quote Post by ephemeral » Mon Jun 10, 2024 2:35 pm I've been trying to make sense of a few things that just don't seem to add up when it comes to Mari. So much is intentionally left unanswered, and this image seemed to be the conclusive answer to a lot of things, but I'm struggling with two key things. Hair color and depicted age in the one image that we get of Yui and this woman with red glasses and red hair. I know that this theory completely breaks the conventional understanding we have around Mari being an OG researcher that is in her late 40's early 50's. But at the end of 3.0+1.0 at the train station when age catches up with all of the pilots I just don't see Mari as being that old. To me she looks around the same age as Shinji. Couple that with her inexplicable ability to pilot Unit 02 and her infatuation with Asuka, to me it seems like the most likely answer would be that she is the child of the original Shikinami (the same way that Shinji is the child of Yui). This would also be in line with the repeating themes of complimentaries and symmetry/balance that we see over and over throughout the series (Angles vs Evas, Longinus vs Cassius, black moon for Lilith and real moon for Adam.) I know that there are gaping plot holes that would need to be filled here, but wanted to see if there is something that I am completely overlooking)

[]: Re: Can I throw this one out there? This has really bothered me for about 2 weeks.             Quote Post by dzzthink » Thu Jun 13, 2024 8:24 am Given the deceptiveness and ambiguity of rebuild, this was probably intentionally made unclear. I mean ambiguity pervades throughout thrice, including what happened to Kaji, are the Reis the same (why she holding Tsubame doll), does Shinji get with Mari in the end etc etc. To me, the woman is an amalgamation of Mari and Asuka characteristics as she has glasses and red hair, respectively. She could be Mari's original or even Asuka's original. I think it's interesting that you say Mari is Asuka's child and I agree with you on many points but I also challenge that with the reverse. The flashbacks suggest a Mari look-a-like was definitely a friend of Shinji's mother. Mari is rather motherly to Asuka such as calling her 'princess', cutting her hair, and saying farewell to her in Thrice. Again, this is intentionally made unclear, and they could also be sisters/half-sisters/no relation.

[]: Re: Can I throw this one out there? This has really bothered me for about 2 weeks.             Quote Post by Konja7 » Fri Jun 14, 2024 9:42 am dzzthink wrote: are the Reis the same (why she holding Tsubame doll)

[]: Re: Can I throw this one out there? This has really bothered me for about 2 weeks.             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Jun 15, 2024 12:00 pm I kinda headcanoned that the Asuka clone series was derived from Maria Iscariot, and they look different because clones just don't look that similar in Eva-verse apparently. Or yea, Mari could be the daughter of the woman Asuka is genetically derived from; either way i always imagined they were directly related in some way. Not that the "piloting Unit 02" thing seems to matter in NTE, because anybody can apparently just pilot anything.

[]: Re: Can I throw this one out there? This has really bothered me for about 2 weeks.             Quote Post by nerv bae » Mon Jun 17, 2024 9:43 am Some discussion of whether the redhead in the AEL photograph is Mari's mother from two years ago: nerv bae wrote: Right, I think that list of three alternatives is the current consensus: 1) She's Mari. 2) She's Mari's biological mother*. 3) She's Mari's original (from whom Mari was cloned). I don't think #1 is a great choice because if the animators wanted her to look just like Mari, they could have drawn her that way. But they didn't! #3 is a bit easier for me to believe than #1 because we can imagine some variation/tuning/error introduced by the cloning process. *random and off-topic: Another theory states that she's not only Mari's biological mother but also Asuka's, because she looks a bit like Asuka too, so maybe that's why there are two men in the photograph -- who AFAIK have no speculated identity at all -- but maybe they're the girls' originals' biological fathers? Anyway, don't mean to start a derail.

[]: Re: Can I throw this one out there? This has really bothered me for about 2 weeks.             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Wed Jun 26, 2024 7:06 am This all makes way more sense when you take the "all pilots are clones and related to the Gods" theory into consideration. But I know it isn't a really popular theory around here. Mari's story is convoluted, but we do know some things. She was already alive when Gendo and Yui met (judging by her personality being the same as in the flashbacks and by Fuyutsuki knowing her in person). She was probably just the first victim of the EVA Curse or doesn't grow at all. Probably the latter, as in HA they say that was her first time piloting an actual Evangelion. I could see her as a test pilot, though. She's not as technical and good as Asuka when she pilots, but she knows a thing or two about it even at the beginning of the film. And yes, I'm familiar with her line to Kaji after that battle. But I don't think it means much, to be honest. It feels like a silly joke she would tell after a tense moment. If anything, she acts way more like an adult in this movie than a child.

[]: Re: Can I throw this one out there? This has really bothered me for about 2 weeks.             Quote Post by unitM » Thu Jun 27, 2024 9:20 pm Thinking about Gendo in this way is interesting too. Gendo - Parent Kaworu - Clone Shinji(?) - Child

[]: Re: Can I throw this one out there? This has really bothered me for about 2 weeks.             Quote Post by AdamMalkovitch » Thu Aug 01, 2024 10:16 pm The red bow is what gets me, that wouldn't be there for no reason and it looks EXACTLY like Asuka's Plug Suit headgear. Remember that people intentionally designed all of this, and considering how dense and purposeful NTE's visuals are, this is very likely supposed to be a connection the audience is intended to make. Until we get that elusive "2.5" story that explores the missing 14 years though, it's unconfirmed, and could even be a red herring. BernardoCairo wrote: She was probably just the first victim of the EVA Curse or doesn't grow at all. Probably the latter, as in HA they say that was her first time piloting an actual Evangelion.

[]: Re: Can I throw this one out there? This has really bothered me for about 2 weeks.             Quote Post by nerv bae » Sat Aug 10, 2024 11:43 am AdamMalkovitch wrote: The red bow is what gets me, that wouldn't be there for no reason and it looks EXACTLY like Asuka's Plug Suit headgear.

[]: Re: Can I throw this one out there? This has really bothered me for about 2 weeks.             Quote Post by ephemeral » Mon Oct 14, 2024 6:19 pm Asuka's headgear is triangular while redhead's is square, right?

[]: Re: Can I throw this one out there? This has really bothered me for about 2 weeks.             Quote Post by Weird_ocean » Wed Oct 16, 2024 2:23 am I made a post on reddit , some time ago, where I suggested a different/alternative view of the whole Originals thing. Maybe I'll post it on Eva geeks someday. It'll be interesting what people here think about it. In short, this:

[]: Re: Can I throw this one out there? This has really bothered me for about 2 weeks.             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Wed Oct 16, 2024 8:36 am I think it would be a way more convoluted solution than to acknowledge how simultaneously Asuka- & Mari-coded that person is and that it had to be intentional. Why would you add glasses tinted in that color and make the hair that shade if not to draw the mind's eye to Mari in some way?

[]: Re: Can I throw this one out there? This has really bothered me for about 2 weeks.             Quote Post by Weird_ocean » Wed Oct 16, 2024 9:23 am Axx°N N. wrote: I think it would be a way more convoluted solution than to acknowledge how simultaneously Asuka- & Mari-coded that person is and that it had to be intentional. Why would you add glasses tinted in that color and make the hair that shade if not to draw the mind's eye to Mari in some way?

[]: Re: Can I throw this one out there? This has really bothered me for about 2 weeks.             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Wed Oct 16, 2024 12:40 pm Weird_ocean wrote: The question is, why one would firstly look at the glasses and not the hairstyle?

[]: Re: Can I throw this one out there? This has really bothered me for about 2 weeks.             Quote Post by Weird_ocean » Wed Oct 16, 2024 1:44 pm Axx°N N. wrote: Well, it's Mari's defining characteristic to the point that they sell her exact model of glasses as merch, and it's emphasized visually throughout the films, not least of which her introductory shot.

[]: Re: Can I throw this one out there? This has really bothered me for about 2 weeks.             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Wed Oct 16, 2024 5:07 pm Weird_ocean wrote: And Asuka's hair is also a very distinct feature. It's just what you're choosing to look at. I still don't understand why she changed her hairstyle in a few years. And why she came back as Mari in 2.22, with her old hairstyle. Seems strange.

[]: Re: Can I throw this one out there? This has really bothered me for about 2 weeks.             Quote Post by Weird_ocean » Thu Oct 17, 2024 1:20 am Axx°N N. wrote: Also, I think I'm more working off some old pre-release theories where it was pretty widely believed that the woman was somehow who both Asuka AND Mari were derived from in some way

[]: Re: Can I throw this one out there? This has really bothered me for about 2 weeks.             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Thu Oct 17, 2024 2:51 am Weird_ocean wrote: So why would she NOT be in his memories?

[]: Re: Can I throw this one out there? This has really bothered me for about 2 weeks.             Quote Post by Weird_ocean » Thu Oct 17, 2024 3:17 am Axx°N N. wrote: Eva is a narrative that in all of its iterations has descended into blatant metatextuality. I don't want to say it's didactic, but it has outright instructed its own reading in several ways. This is the franchise where, in EoE, Rei and Kaworu have speaking lines about "being the hope that people will one day understand each other" and "being the words 'I love you.'" There are plot-based reasons for the fantasmagoria, but it's a literal plot device that begins as an allowance for abstract metaphor and then procedes to deconstruct and erase its own physical limitations so that the metaphor exists on its own, concrete within and outside of the story world simultaneously. It's Brechtian by way of sci-fi. Hope literally and metaphorically creates a spear, "reality and fiction overlap."

[]: Re: Can I throw this one out there? This has really bothered me for about 2 weeks.             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Thu Oct 17, 2024 1:14 pm Weird_ocean wrote: Another thing Evangelion is known for, is for giving the viewer hints and clues in the story, to build the narrative. So saying, "It's just a metaphor." is an easy way to disregard the logic and words of your opponent. That's why it's a fallacy. If I'm talking about the lore, and you're talking about the metaphor, it looks like we're discussing the same thing, but we're not anymore. It's just logically inconsistent conversation.
